 49→/**
 50→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 51→ *
 52→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 53→ */
 54→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 55→    StandardRule(
 56→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 57→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 58→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 59→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 60→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 61→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 62→            ),
 63→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 64→        ),
 65→    ),
 66→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 74→
 75→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 76→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→    ) {
 84→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 85→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 86→        when (node.elementType) {
 87→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            else -> Unit
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 98→        node: ASTNode,
 99→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        val inspectNode = node
103→            .children()
104→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
105→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
106→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
107→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
108→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
109→            emit = emit,
110→        )
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
114→        node: ASTNode,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117→    ) {
118→        val inspectNode = node
119→            .children()
120→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
121→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
122→            return
123→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
124→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
125→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
126→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
127→            emit = emit,
128→        )
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun visitValueList(
132→        node: ASTNode,
133→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
134→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
135→    ) {
136→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
137→            node
138→                .children()
139→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
140→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
141→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
143→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
144→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
145→                        emit = emit,
146→                    )
147→                }
148→        }
149→    }
150→
151→    private fun visitTypeList(
152→        node: ASTNode,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        val inspectNode = node
157→            .children()
158→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
159→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
160→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
161→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
162→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
163→            emit = emit,
164→        )
165→    }
166→
167→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
168→        node: ASTNode,
169→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
170→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
171→    ) {
172→        val psi = node.psi
173→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
174→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
175→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
176→            return
177→        }
178→
179→        val inspectNode = node
180→            .children()
181→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
182→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
183→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
184→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
185→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
186→            emit = emit,
187→        )
188→    }
189→    private fun visitClass(
190→        node: ASTNode,
191→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
192→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
193→    ) {
194→        val psi = node.psi
195→        require(psi is KtClass)
196→
197→        node
198→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
199→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
200→            ?.takeUnless {
201→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
202→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
203→            }?.let { classBody ->
204→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
205→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
206→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
207→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
208→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
209→                    emit = emit,
210→                )
211→            }
212→    }
213→
214→    /**
215→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
216→     *
217→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
218→     * last element of the class.
219→     */
220→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
221→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
222→
223→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
224→            .children()
225→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
226→
227→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
228→    }
229→
230→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
231→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
232→            children()
233→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
234→                .toList()
235→                .takeLast(2)
236→
237→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
238→    }
239→
240→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
241→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
242→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→    ) {
246→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
247→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
248→        val trailingCommaState = when {
249→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
250→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
251→        }
252→        when (trailingCommaState) {
253→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
254→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
255→                    inspectNode
256→                        .treeParent
257→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
258→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
259→                        ?.prevLeaf()
260→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
261→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
262→                                emit(
263→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
264→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
265→                                    true,
266→                                )
267→                                if (autoCorrect) {
268→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
269→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
270→                                }
271→                            }
272→                        }
273→                } else {
274→                    emit(
275→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
276→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
277→                        true,
278→                    )
279→                    if (autoCorrect) {
280→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
281→                    }
282→                }
283→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
284→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
285→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
286→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
287→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
288→                        emit(
289→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
290→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
291→                            true,
292→                        )
293→                    } else {
294→                        emit(
295→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
296→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
297→                            true,
298→                        )
299→                    }
300→                    if (autoCorrect) {
301→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
302→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
303→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
304→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
305→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
306→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
307→                            } else {
308→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
309→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
310→                            }
311→                        }
312→
313→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
323→                                    add(newline)
324→                                    add(createSemicolon())
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                            Unit
328→                        } else {
329→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
330→                        }
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
354→        when {
355→            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
356→                isMultiline(element.parent)
357→            }
358→            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
359→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
360→            }
361→            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
362→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
363→            }
364→            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
365→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
366→            }
367→            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
368→                element.children.size == 1 &&
369→                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
370→                // special handling for collection literal
371→                // @Annotation([
372→                //    "something",
373→                // ])
374→                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
375→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
376→            }
377→            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
378→                false

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→
 17→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
 18→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
 19→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
 20→
 21→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
 22→
 23→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 24→
 25→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 26→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 27→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 28→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 29→
 30→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 31→
 32→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 33→
 34→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 35→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 36→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 37→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 38→
 39→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 40→
 41→#### Experimental rules
 42→
 43→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 44→
 45→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 46→
 47→#### EditorConfig
 48→
 49→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 50→
 51→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 52→
 53→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 54→
 55→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 56→
 57→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 58→
 59→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 60→
 61→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 62→
 63→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 64→
 65→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 66→
 67→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 68→
 69→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 70→
 71→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 72→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfigProperties                  |
 75→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 76→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.UsesEditorConfigProperties |
 77→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 78→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 79→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 80→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 81→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 82→
 83→
 84→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 85→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 86→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 87→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 88→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 89→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 90→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 91→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 92→
 93→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 94→
 95→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 96→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

 98→
 99→

content.text:
The issue was that `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` would crash with a `NoSuchElementException` when encountering an empty enum class. This happened because the rule logic assumed that if an enum class has a body, it must contain at least one enum entry when trying to determine where to place or check for a trailing comma.

I investigated the `visitClass` method in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt` and identified that the crash occurs in `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` which calls `children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }`. For an empty enum class, there are no children matching `KtEnumEntry`.

I fixed the issue by adding a check in `visitClass` to ensure that the class body actually contains enum entries before proceeding with the trailing comma logic.

I verified the fix by creating a reproduction test case with an empty enum class, which initially failed (crashed) and then passed after the fix. I also ran the existing tests for `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` to ensure no regressions were introduced.

Finally, I updated `CHANGELOG.md` to document the fix.

Summary of changes:
1.  Modified `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`: Added a check `it.children().any { child -> child.psi is KtEnumEntry }` in `visitClass` to skip processing if the enum class has no entries.
2.  Modified `CHANGELOG.md`: Added an entry under the "Fixed" section.

 74→
 75→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {

 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→    ) {
 84→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 85→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 86→        when (node.elementType) {
 87→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            else -> Unit
 94→        }
 95→    }

 98→        node: ASTNode,
 99→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        val inspectNode = node
103→            .children()
104→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
105→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
106→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
107→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),

138→                .children()

159→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(

241→        inspectNode: ASTNode,

243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

245→    ) {

259→                        ?.prevLeaf()
260→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->

264→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
265→                                    true,
266→                                )

268→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()

272→                        }

274→                    emit(
275→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
276→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
277→                        true,
278→                    )
279→                    if (autoCorrect) {

289→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
290→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",

293→                    } else {
294→                        emit(
295→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
296→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
297→                            true,
298→                        )
299→                    }
300→                    if (autoCorrect) {
301→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
302→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()

309→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)

314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {

318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
323→                                    add(newline)
324→                                    add(createSemicolon())
325→                                }

329→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
330→                        }
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }

344→        }

347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
354→        when {
355→            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
356→                isMultiline(element.parent)
357→            }
358→            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
359→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
360→            }
361→            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
362→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
363→            }
364→            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
365→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
366→            }
367→            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
368→                element.children.size == 1 &&
369→                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
370→                // special handling for collection literal
371→                // @Annotation([
372→                //    "something",
373→                // ])
374→                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
375→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
376→            }
377→            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
378→                false
379→            }
380→            else -> {
381→                element.textContains('\n')
382→            }
383→        }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
386→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
387→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
388→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
389→        } else {
390→            false
391→        }

393→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
394→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
395→            this
396→        } else {
397→            prevCodeLeaf()
398→        }
399→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
400→    }
401→
402→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
403→        from: PsiElement,
404→        to: PsiElement,
405→    ): Boolean {
406→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
407→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
408→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
409→                return true
410→            }
411→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
412→        }
413→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
414→    }

416→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
417→        /**
418→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
419→         */
420→        EXISTS,

422→        /**
423→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
424→         */
425→        MISSING,

429→         */
430→        NOT_EXISTS,
431→
432→        /**
433→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
434→         */
435→        REDUNDANT,

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→
 17→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
 18→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
 19→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
 20→
 21→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
 22→
 23→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 24→
 25→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 26→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 27→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 28→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 29→
 30→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 31→
 32→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 33→
 34→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 35→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 36→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 37→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 38→
 39→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 40→
 41→#### Experimental rules
 42→
 43→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 44→
 45→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 46→
 47→#### EditorConfig
 48→
 49→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 50→
 51→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 52→
 53→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 54→
 55→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 56→
 57→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 58→
 59→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 60→
 61→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 62→
 63→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 64→
 65→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 66→
 67→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 68→
